demonstrates: analog VHS noise floor and snow — luma/chroma static, dropout speckle, and head-switching noise texture from a real tape with no input signal (640x480, ~6m21s, CC0 by Caleb Minear via Wikimedia Commons)

VHS artifacts over real picture content — chroma bleed, soft luma, tracking error, bottom-of-frame head-switching band, dropouts, and generational/composite loss from a 1989 Palace Video budget VHS rip of public-domain cartoons

CRT mask/subpixel geometry ground truth — shadow-mask RGB phosphor triads (dot/slot grid) vs Trinitron aperture-grille continuous vertical RGB stripes. URL-cited only (CC-BY-SA share-alike → not bundled in the commercial product).

real low-bitrate codec artifacts — 8x8 DCT block edges, mosquito/ringing noise around high-contrast edges, and quantization banding, produced by re-encoding the public-domain CEP507 clip at 90kbps MPEG-4 via ffmpeg. Derived internal reference (method reproducible from the PD source); not bundled.
